hot_topics.topic: FIFA's controversial Balogun decision ignites World Cup furor
hot_topics.synthesis: FIFA's decision to lift US striker Folarin Balogun's one-match suspension ahead of the World Cup match against Belgium has sparked widespread criticism. The Belgian football federation and European soccer body UEFA formally challenged the ruling, deeming it 'incomprehensible and unjustifiable.' Reports indicate direct lobbying from the US government, including President Donald Trump and Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick, raising concerns about political influence compromising FIFA's integrity.

hot_topics.topic: Microsoft announces layoffs impacting Xbox and sales divisions
hot_topics.synthesis: Microsoft has commenced a new round of layoffs, affecting approximately 4,800 employees, which accounts for 2.1% of its global workforce. These job reductions are primarily concentrated within the company's commercial sales business and the Xbox division. The company attributes these cuts to evolving industry dynamics and the growing influence of AI on its operations.

hot_topics.topic: Massive crowds gather for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's funeral in Iran
hot_topics.synthesis: Huge crowds have converged on Tehran to participate in the funeral procession for Iran's late supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who died four months prior in US-Israeli strikes. The event has been characterized as a significant display orchestrated by Iran for a global audience, with many mourners waving Iranian flags and red banners symbolizing vengeance.

hot_topics.topic: Ukraine faces missile shortages amid Russian attacks and strategic strikes
hot_topics.synthesis: Ukraine is grappling with a shortage of interceptor missiles following a 'massive Russian attack' on Kyiv that killed 23 and severely damaged a residential building. Simultaneously, Ukrainian forces have intensified strategic strikes deep into annexed Crimea and other Russian territories. These Ukrainian operations are causing an energy crisis and political challenges within Russia, highlighting the evolving dynamics of the conflict.

hot_topics.topic: Smart glasses: privacy concerns meet booming industry investment
hot_topics.synthesis: The rapid advancement and adoption of smart glasses technology are raising significant privacy concerns, with commentators highlighting potential infringements as depicted in popular media. Despite these ethical debates, the smart glasses sector continues to attract substantial investment. Companies like Even Realities, which develops camera-free smart glasses, have recently achieved billion-dollar valuations.

hot_topics.topic: Trump and Mamdani deliver contrasting July 4th visions for America
hot_topics.synthesis: President Donald Trump and New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani delivered markedly contrasting speeches over the Fourth of July weekend, offering vastly different visions for America's future on its 250th anniversary. Pundits noted the stark divergence in their tones and content, reflecting deep political divisions within the nation.

hot_topics.topic: UK political figures engage with England's World Cup campaign
hot_topics.synthesis: Leading UK political figures, including Prime Minister Keir Starmer and his expected successor Andy Burnham, have been closely involved with England's World Cup campaign. Starmer intervened with FIFA to prevent an earlier kickoff for the England vs. Mexico match and approved extended pub opening hours for late-night viewing, a move the Liberal Democrats claim credit for. Both Starmer and Burnham watched the team's early morning victory, expressing national pride.

items.title: Begone dull care
items.summary: A 1949 short film titled 'Begone Dull Care' merges jazz music with innovative abstract animation, creating a sensory experience. The piece invites viewers to let its exuberant visuals interpret the accompanying music.

items.title: Skill nostalgia
items.summary: The essay 'Skill nostalgia' explores whether the increasing interest in hands-on activities like beekeeping and baking represents a mere escapist fantasy or a foundational shift towards a more human-centric approach to work. It delves into the radical reasons behind the desire to create things by hand.

items.title: Bentley teases its first EV, the Torcal
items.summary: Bentley has announced its first electric vehicle will be named the Torcal, continuing its tradition of using names from natural landmarks. The name's Latin root 'torquere' hints at the immense, effortless torque expected from an electrified powertrain.

items.title: F1 in Britain: Automated software to blame for crushing expectations
items.summary: Formula 1's British Grand Prix saw reliability issues again plague the fastest car, while a software error mistakenly announced a late-race restart. The incident turned what was an entertaining race into a 'damp squib' for many viewers and commentators.

items.title: The Czinger 21C might be the wildest car we drive all year
items.summary: The Czinger 21C is a hybrid hypercar boasting extreme specifications, including a 1,250 hp twin-turbo V8 engine and a 0-60 mph time of 1.92 seconds. This carbon-fiber-bodied vehicle recently set lap records at five California racetracks.

items.title: UK regulator warns of "arms race" to keep up with AI use in financial services
items.summary: A senior UK official from the Financial Conduct Authority warns that regulators are in an 'arms race' to keep pace with AI in financial services. He urged authorities to review whether large language models should fall under their regulatory scope.

items.title: Open Thread 441
items.summary: The weekly 'Open Thread 441' on Astral Codex Ten includes announcements for grantmaking.ai's $1M fund for AI existential risk projects and a protest by StopTheRace.AI against AI development at San Francisco tech offices. It also mentions community resources for ACX readers.

items.title: 'Definitely felt a big bang': Delta plane hit by firework while landing in Chicago
items.summary: A Delta plane carrying 52 passengers and six crew was struck by a firework while landing in Chicago, with passengers reporting a 'big bang.' Fortunately, no injuries were reported during the incident.

items.title: 'I ate ketchup and cheese,' says Venezuelan girl trapped under quake rubble for 32 hours
items.summary: A 12-year-old Venezuelan girl named Fabiana survived 32 hours trapped under earthquake rubble by consuming ketchup and cheese. She was rescued from a collapsed 10-storey residential building after two powerful earthquakes struck Venezuela.

items.title: 'The spectacle Iran wants the world to see': Lyse Doucet in Tehran
items.summary: Huge crowds have lined the streets of Tehran for the funeral procession of Iran's late supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

items.title: A global hub for fake luxury goods, Vietnam cracks down on its black market
items.summary: Vietnam, identified as a significant hub for fake luxury goods, is escalating its crackdown on the black market following pressure from the Trump administration. Locals remain divided on the effectiveness and impact of these new enforcement efforts.

items.title: Australian PM apologises for 'inappropriate' comments about Kylie Minogue
items.summary: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has issued an 'unequivocal' apology for 'inappropriate' comments he made about popstar Kylie Minogue. His remarks had previously sparked a public backlash and criticism.

items.title: Bangladesh courts China even as ties with India improve
items.summary: Bangladesh is actively strengthening its relationship with China, even as diplomatic ties with India, which had turned frosty under the interim government, are improving. This indicates a complex geopolitical strategy of balancing regional powers.

items.title: From 'a beautiful night' to 'world's greatest hangover', guests react to Taylor and Travis's wedding
items.summary: Famous guests have shared their reactions on social media to the wedding of pop star Taylor Swift and football player Travis Kelce in New York, describing it as everything from 'a beautiful night' to 'the world's greatest hangover.' The highly publicized event drew significant attention.

items.title: Girl trapped in Venezuela quake 'survived on ketchup and cheese'
items.summary: A 12-year-old girl named Fabiana miraculously survived being trapped for 32 hours after Venezuela's devastating earthquakes, sustaining herself on ketchup and cheese. International correspondent Yogita Limayee met the resilient survivor to share her story.

items.title: Huge crowds fill Tehran streets for Khamenei's funeral procession
items.summary: Many people were waving Iranian flags and red banners symbolising vengeance for the former supreme leader's war death.

items.title: Is this a breakthrough moment for soccer in the US?
items.summary: The victory of the US national soccer team against Belgium could mark a breakthrough moment for the sport's growth and popularity among American audiences. This win might signify further strides in establishing soccer's prominence in the United States.

items.title: Marine Le Pen appeal verdict: Why this moment matters for France
items.summary: The verdict on Marine Le Pen's appeal holds significant political weight for France, as the National Rally leader currently tops opinion polls for the 2027 presidential election. The outcome will determine her eligibility to stand, shaping the country's political landscape.

items.title: Sri Lanka prison riots leave 26 dead and more than 100 injured
items.summary: Two days of violence at Negombo Prison in Sri Lanka have resulted in 26 deaths and over 100 injuries, making it the country's worst prison riot in years. Authorities are investigating the cause and aftermath of the unrest.

items.title: Super Typhoon Bavi strikes US Pacific islands with pummeling winds
items.summary: Super Typhoon Bavi has severely impacted the US Pacific islands, lashing the Mariana Islands and prompting emergency warnings on Guam. The storm brought pummeling winds, causing significant disruption to the region.

items.title: The African fishermen who blame Chinese trawlers for their woes
items.summary: Fishermen in Sierra Leone are accusing large Chinese trawlers of illegally depleting local fish stocks, severely impacting their livelihoods and the marine environment. The presence of these foreign vessels is causing significant distress among African fishing communities.

items.title: Thousands of forgotten WW1 soldiers recognised for first time
items.summary: Nearly 10,000 previously unrecognised soldiers from World War I are being added to the official database of war dead, a long-awaited recognition for their descendants. This cherished step aims to honor those who were largely forgotten for decades.

items.title: Trump confirms he asked Fifa to review Balogun ban
items.summary: President Donald Trump confirms he asked Fifa to review United States striker Folarin Balogun's one-match suspension at the World Cup.

items.title: Ukraine warns of interceptor missile shortage as 23 killed in Kyiv region
items.summary: President Zelensky says Sunday's "massive Russian attack" on Kyiv consisted of 68 missiles and 351 strike drones.

items.title: Watch: Russian attack leaves gaping hole in Kyiv residential building
items.summary: The BBC's Sarah Rainsford is at the scene of one of the blasts to hit Ukraine's capital overnight.

items.title: Watch: Why Australia's PM apologised for 'inappropriate' Kylie comments
items.summary: A video explains why Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese offered an apology for remarks he made about pop star Kylie Minogue. His comments in a podcast interview had previously drawn public criticism for being 'inappropriate'.

items.title: Why an anti-sacrilege law in India's Punjab has sparked controversy
items.summary: An anti-sacrilege law introduced in India's Punjab state has sparked widespread controversy, rekindling sensitive political and religious issues ahead of next year's state elections. The legislation has ignited debate over freedom of expression and religious sentiment.

items.title: Why Ukrainian strikes on annexed Crimea are a blow to Putin
items.summary: Eastern Europe correspondent Sarah Rainsford explains why Crimea is a target for Kyiv, and why the peninsula matters to Vladimir Putin.

items.title: Wildfire in southern France forces evacuation of 10,000 people
items.summary: Tour de France organisers ban spectators from stage three as a wildfire hits the Pyrénées-Orientales region.

items.title: Building relationships with customers through support didn't turn out as hoped
items.summary: An article reflects on the unexpected challenges encountered when attempting to build strong customer relationships primarily through support interactions. It details lessons learned from a podcast app's experience in fostering loyalty this way.

items.title: GPT-5.6 Sol Ultra will be in Codex
items.summary: A social media post indicates that GPT-5.6 Sol Ultra will be integrated into Codex, an OpenAI project focused on translating natural language into code. This suggests an enhancement of AI capabilities in code generation.

items.title: Has_not_been_viewed_much
items.summary: An online note titled 'Has_not_been_viewed_much' explores themes related to visibility and audience engagement in the digital space. It offers reflections on content that might be overlooked.

items.title: Nintendo announces new product revisions in Europe with replaceable batteries
items.summary: Nintendo has announced upcoming revisions to its Switch products in Europe, which will include replaceable batteries. This move aims to enhance the sustainability and longevity of its gaming hardware.

items.title: Real-time map of Great Britain's rail network
items.summary: A real-time interactive map visualizing Great Britain's rail network is now available, offering a dynamic overview of train movements. The tool provides a detailed, up-to-the-minute representation of the country's railway system.

items.title: Road to Elm 1.0
items.summary: An article discusses the ongoing progress towards the release of Elm 1.0, highlighting specific improvements focused on achieving faster build times. This update aims to enhance the developer experience for the functional programming language.

items.title: South Korea’s hottest new bachelors are chip workers
items.summary: South Korean semiconductor workers are becoming highly desirable marriage partners due to substantial bonuses from the booming AI chip industry, creating a new 'silicon-collar' elite. This newfound wealth is reshaping social dynamics and deepening concerns about economic inequality in the country.

items.title: No, China did not manage to avoid a crash
items.summary: The article argues that China did experience an economic crash despite official reports of uninterrupted growth, citing alternative indicators like surging youth unemployment and a dramatic fall in housing construction. While China's government redirected credit to manufacturing to maintain growth, this strategy may lead to an overhang of unproductive 'zombie' companies.

items.title: Hate food waste? 7 creative ways to turn your leftovers into a new meal
items.summary: This article presents seven creative recipes contributed by readers, offering innovative ways to transform leftovers into new meals. The aim is to help reduce food waste and inspire sustainable cooking practices at home.

items.title: Historic World Cup furor at 'incomprehensible' FIFA decision to let U.S. forward Balogun play
items.summary: The Belgian soccer federation is challenging FIFA's ruling to let Balogun play despite getting a red card in his previous game. Belgium's statement came just 11 hours before kickoff in Seattle. European soccer body UEFA criticizes FIFA's "incomprehensible and unjustifiable decisi…

items.title: Huge crowds of mourners join a funeral procession for Iran's Ayatollah Ali Khamenei
items.summary:
Iran holds a funeral procession for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei more than four months after he was killed in U.S.-Israeli strikes.
(Image credit: Atta Kenare)

items.title: Man sues DHS after agents tracked him down for sending a scathing email to ICE
items.summary: David Streever is suing the Department of Homeland Security after federal agents tracked him and issued a warning for a critical email he sent to the former head of ICE. The agents suggested his email could be illegal, prompting concerns about free speech.

items.title: Militant LGBTQ+ rights group 'the Lavender Panthers' was founded on this day in 1973
items.summary: 'Reverend Ray' Broshears founded the militant LGBTQ+ rights group, the Lavender Panthers, on this day in 1973. The vigilante organization's actions and impact are still recognized and felt in contemporary LGBTQ+ activism.

items.title: These Medicare beneficiaries thought their drug plan was free. Then they lost it
items.summary: Thousands of Medicare beneficiaries unexpectedly lost their drug coverage over small delinquent payments, after previously free plans began charging premiums without their knowledge. Most now cannot regain coverage until 2027, highlighting a critical flaw in communication.

items.title: Trump, Mamdani give contrasting July 4th speeches. And, U.S. faces Belgium in World Cup
items.summary:
For the nation's 250th birthday, President Trump and NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani had opposing visions of America. And, the U.S. and Belgium face off today in the FIFA World Cup.
(Image credit: Alex Wroblewski)

items.title: Under Trump, spouses of U.S. citizens face policy changes in the immigration system
items.summary: Under the Trump administration, spouses of US citizens are facing significant policy changes within the immigration system, losing the traditionally special status they once held. Immigration lawyers note that their path to citizenship has become more scrutinized and difficult.

items.title: Belgian's Beltway fans
items.summary:
WASHINGTON — As all eyes are on Seattle, Belgian fans will gather not far from the source of the day’s real intrigue — the White House — for tonight’s match.
Staffers from the Belgian Embassy will be at Wunder Garten, a trendy outdoor beer garden in the city’s NoMa neighborhood,…

items.title: Belgians formally challenge Balogun decision
items.summary:
The Royal Belgian Football Association has formally challenged FIFA’s decision to withdraw the one-match suspension of U.S. striker Folarin Balogun, contesting his eligibility to play in tonight's match between Belgium and the United States.
“The RBFA has still not received eithe…

items.title: Belgians weigh Folarin Balogun as 'darling of the match' in which he didn't play
items.summary:
BRUSSELS — FIFA's decision to lift U.S. striker Folarin Balogun's one-match ban — freeing him up to play against Belgium in Monday's World Cup round-of-16 match on Monday this week — has not gone down well in the European nation.
Coverage from Belgian national broadcaster RTBF of…

items.title: Can Gianni Infantino keep winning over Trump without losing the world?
items.summary:
Our Sophia Cai wrote in December about concerns within FIFA that President Gianni Infantino was compromising the credibility of soccer's governing body in his efforts to placate Donald Trump:
Read Sophia's story "Gianni Infantino's Trump Problem" here
.

items.title: Dallas police officials trade gifts with Egyptians after fracas
items.summary: Dallas police officials met with staff from the Egyptian World Cup team to smooth over relations after an altercation where an officer shoved a team manager. The meeting involved an exchange of gifts, with police emphasizing a commitment to respect and understanding.

items.title: European soccer leaders make plans to take Belgium's side against FIFA
items.summary: FIFA’s decision to suspend Folarin Balogun’s one-match ban has sparked anger inside European soccer, with both continental confederation UEFA and the Royal Belgian Football Association discussing possible next steps, according to a high-ranking UEFA official. The official, who wa…

items.title: Governing on low power
items.summary:
LONDON — U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer is feeling “sleep-deprived” after watching England beat World Cup co-hosts Mexico in the small hours this morning.
Starmer watched the game — which kicked off at 2 a.m. London time — in his Downing Street flat with his teenage son.
“He’s…

items.title: Inside Lutnick's back channel with FIFA brass
items.summary:
Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick was involved in behind-the-scenes conversations with FIFA to get U.S. striker Folarin Balogun’s red-card ban suspended, according to two people familiar with the matter granted anonymity to discuss private deliberations.
Lutnick has developed clo…

items.title: Keir Starmer helped stop earlier England vs. Mexico kickoff
items.summary:
Keir Starmer wants to bring football home before he leaves No. 10 Downing Street.
The British prime minister intervened to help stop FIFA from rescheduling England's football match in Mexico City by several hours, keeping the kickoff set for 1 a.m. Monday morning U.K. time. FIFA…

items.title: Starmer, Burnham to skip pubs
items.summary:
LONDON — The U.K.'s current and next prime minister will both skip the pub to watch England's match against Mexico at their homes, advisers confirmed.
Keir Starmer — the outgoing PM who promoted his move to let English pubs stay open till 5 a.m. for the late-night kickoff — will…

items.title: Suspension suspended
items.summary:
Donald Trump on Sunday afternoon thanked FIFA after it suspended a one-match ban for U.S. star Folarin Balogun, allowing him to play in Monday's Last 16 matchup against Belgium.
"Thank you to FIFA for doing what was right, and reversing a great injustice!" Trump posted on Truth S…

items.title: The connection between Oasis, England and Andy Burnham
items.summary: England football fans are adopting Oasis' 'Wonderwall' as a new anthem at the World Cup, a song linked to Andy Burnham, Britain’s prime minister-in-waiting, who uses Oasis music in his campaigns. Burnham hopes to enter Downing Street on a wave of national euphoria if England wins.

items.title: The other US-Belgian spat
items.summary: US Ambassador to Belgium Bill White asserted that Brussels' Parc du Cinquantenaire was left immaculate after lavish Independence Day festivities, amidst Belgian authorities investigating potential fireworks damage to a historic building. White stated his contractor would remediate any required repairs.

items.title: UK's Lib Dems say they scored on pubs' added time
items.summary:
LONDON — England may not prevail against Mexico at the Estadio Azteca tonight, but the United Kingdom’s Liberal Democratic Party thinks it has already won the week.
The third-largest party in Westminster is claiming that it delivered late pub-closing times after pressuring Prime…

items.title: Up next for the DSA? Two major swing states.
items.summary: The Democratic Socialists of America (DSA) are shifting resources to Michigan and Wisconsin primaries, aiming to translate recent deep-blue wins into competitive statewide races. Key figures like Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez and Twitch streamer Hasan Piker are set to campaign for DSA-backed candidates.

items.title: What's really at stake between Mexico and England
items.summary: Beyond the World Cup match, Mexico is keen to restart stalled trade talks with Britain, focusing on sectors like pharmaceuticals and financial services. The two nations are seeking to bolster their economic relationship, which currently operates under a rolled-over post-Brexit deal.

items.title: Researchers Reveal the Power of ‘Quantum Proofs’
items.summary: Researchers have unveiled new insights into the capabilities of 'quantum proofs,' further demonstrating how hypothetical quantum computers could significantly outperform classical machines in solving complex mathematical problems. This research contributes to understanding the power dynamics between quantum and classical computing.

items.title: sqlite-utils 4.0rc3
items.summary: Simon Willison announced the release candidate 3 for sqlite-utils 4.0, highlighting significant new features like support for introspecting and creating compound foreign keys. The update also aligns with SQLite's convention for case-insensitive column names.

items.title: Sunday Thread + Mailbag
items.summary: This article serves as a weekly open thread for readers to post questions and engage in discussions. It functions as a forum for community interaction and diverse topics.

items.title: Amazon will stop accepting new customers for Mechanical Turk
items.summary: Amazon has announced it will no longer accept new customers for its Mechanical Turk crowdsourcing platform, signaling a potential winding down of the service. This change marks a significant shift for the long-standing micro-task marketplace.

items.title: Apple brings back card payments for Apple Account purchases in India after a four-year hiatus
items.summary: Apple has initiated a phased reintroduction of card payments for Apple Account purchases in India. This follows a four-year hiatus, after the company successfully adapted its payment systems to comply with India's local regulations.

items.title: How to reserve and change your WhatsApp username
items.summary: WhatsApp is enabling users to reserve usernames in anticipation of a full rollout, which will allow connections without sharing a phone number. This feature aims to provide greater privacy and convenience for users.

items.title: Microsoft lays off nearly 5,000 employees across Xbox, commercial sales
items.summary: Microsoft cut around 4,800 roles, or 2.1% of its global workforce, on Monday — the latest in a series of layoffs that’s stoking fears of AI replacing jobs. The layoffs will hit Xbox and commercial sales the hardest.

items.title: Reddit is using LLMs to solve a problem LLMs largely created
items.summary: Reddit is deploying large language models (LLMs) to combat the surge of spam on its platform, a problem largely exacerbated by the proliferation of AI-generated content. This strategy involves fighting AI-fueled spam with AI itself.

items.title: Smart glasses maker Even Realities hits $1B valuation with $150M funding led by Meituan, Tencent
items.summary: Even Realities, an ex-Apple team building camera-free smart glasses, raised $150 million from Meituan and Tencent at a $1 billion valuation.

items.title: Station F ramps up as a launchpad for Europe’s hottest AI startups
items.summary: Station F, a prominent Paris-based startup hub, is expanding its F/ai accelerator program to solidify its position as a launchpad for leading AI startups in Europe. This initiative aims to foster innovation and growth within the European AI ecosystem.

items.title: This humanoid robotics company is going public, but its CEO isn’t promising a robot in your home anytime soon
items.summary: Agility Robotics, a humanoid robotics company, is going public via a SPAC, prioritizing execution and a cautious approach over immediate consumer robot deployment. Its CEO emphasizes a long-term strategy rather than promising in-home robots soon.

items.title: Trump memecoin investors lost $3.8 billion, analysis finds
items.summary: An analysis revealed that nearly 1 million investors collectively lost $3.8 billion after purchasing President Donald Trump’s $TRUMP memecoin. In contrast, Trump himself is reported to have earned $636 million from the cryptocurrency.

items.title: Uber’s European expansion plans may have hit a speed bump
items.summary: Uber's ambitious plans to expand into seven new European markets in 2026 have reportedly encountered obstacles, with launches in five of those locations now on hold. The reasons for this slowdown were not immediately detailed.

items.title: Arthur Brooks: Taylor and Travis, a Prenup Won’t Protect Your Marriage
items.summary: Arthur Brooks critiques the growing trend of prenuptial agreements, exemplified by Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's sophisticated prenup, questioning their role in fostering marital trust and intimacy. He suggests that negotiating divorce terms before marriage may undermine the foundation of a relationship.

items.title: Dr. Cornel West: Is America Living Up to Its Promise?
items.summary: Dr. Cornel West offered a reflective perspective on America's 250th anniversary, posing the question of whether the nation is truly living up to its foundational promises. His insights provide a deeper analysis beyond mere celebration or condemnation of the country's history.

items.title: Is Ukraine Winning the War?
items.summary:
Conventional wisdom regarding the war in Ukraine tends to swing like a pendulum, generally in tandem with the fortunes of the war. That means today’s optimism regarding Ukraine’s improved strategic prospects, while in many ways justified, is missing some balance.
First, the good…

items.title: Two Nations, Under Trump and Mamdani
items.summary:
Will the real America please stand up?
This Fourth of July weekend, in between barbecues and fireworks and celebrations galore, the country was treated, naturally, to two speeches by our headliner politicians: New York City mayor Zohran Mamdani and President Donald Trump. The spe…

items.title: Can Partiful keep the party going?
items.summary: Partiful, a startup that gained traction by providing stipends for college students to host parties on its app, filled a void left by the decline of Facebook events. The company aimed to simplify party planning and RSVPs for a new generation.

items.title: Former Xbox studios Double Fine and Compulsion will keep games after going indie
items.summary: Microsoft is spinning off four Xbox game studios, with Double Fine Productions and Compulsion Games transitioning to independent studios. Both Double Fine and Compulsion will retain ownership of their franchises and game catalogs.

items.title: I spy
items.summary: The article explores the privacy challenges posed by smart glasses, drawing parallels with a Netflix show where such devices lead to privacy infringements. It argues that Hollywood has inadvertently highlighted the significant cultural problem with current smart glasses.

items.title: Microsoft is laying off 4,800 employees
items.summary:
Satya Nadella in February 2026. | Photo by Sven Hoppe / picture alliance via Getty Images
A year after cutting around
9,100 employees
, Microsoft is making further layoffs today as it begins its new financial year. The software maker is laying off around 4,800 employees today, ap…

items.title: Mr. Lif’s Emergency Rations EP is post-9/11 hip-hop at its most daring
items.summary: Mr. Lif's 2002 'Emergency Rations' EP is reviewed as a daring example of post-9/11 hip-hop, showcasing his politically conscious style within the experimental Definitive Jux label. The EP served as a precursor to his full-length album, 'I Phantom'.

items.title: Nintendo will stop selling the original Switch in Europe next year
items.summary: Nintendo has confirmed it will discontinue sales of all original Nintendo Switch family systems in Europe starting mid-February 2027. This move comes almost ten years after the console's initial launch.

items.title: Some of the nation’s rich are letting AI teach their kids
items.summary: Some affluent American families are turning to AI to educate their children, enrolling them in programs like Forge Prep and Alpha School that charge tens of thousands of dollars for AI tutors. This trend is notably prevalent among Silicon Valley residents.

items.title: The DJI Osmo Pocket 3 lookalike is down to $329
items.summary: The Xtra Muse portable camera, a close lookalike of the DJI Osmo Pocket 3, is currently on sale for $329, its lowest price yet. This vlogging camera now supports wireless Bluetooth mics after a firmware update, making it a competitive option.
